# NTLSN — National Teaching and Learning Support Network

> A free, open commons connecting Australia's higher-education teaching and learning sector: every conference, showcase, workshop and professional-development opportunity across all 42 universities, plus a rescued 31-year archive of nationally funded teaching scholarship. No logins, no paywalls. Licensed CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Founded by A/Prof Seb Dianati (University of Southern Queensland).

## Core
- [NTLSN home](https://www.ntlsn.com): the full platform — interactive sector map, events calendar, resources, and recognition tools across all 42 Australian universities.
- [Sector Archive](https://www.ntlsn.com/#ntlsn-archive): 1,431 ALTC- and OLT-funded learning and teaching works, 1994–2025. NTLSN indexes titles and authors; full text lives at the Australian Government Learning & Teaching Repository.
- [Best Practice guides](https://www.ntlsn.com/#ntlsn-bestpractice): 80 curated Good Practice Reports and guides, 2006–2024 (academic integrity, work-integrated learning, assessment, curriculum design, student transition).

## Free in-browser tools
- [ATEC · TEQSA Curriculum QA](https://www.ntlsn.com/atec-curriculum-qa.html): self-check a course's outcomes against its AQF level (Bloom's verbs), ATEC mission-compact alignment (widening participation, VET→HE), and the GenAI-resilience of its assessments. Indicative, in-browser.
- [Course Quality Self-Check](https://www.ntlsn.com/selfcheck.html): rate a course against the TELAS standards and UDL guidelines; produces a readiness profile.
- [TEQSA GenAI Readiness](https://www.ntlsn.com/teqsa-readiness.html): self-assess a course or program against TEQSA generative-AI guidance.
- [Teaching Recognition Passport](https://www.ntlsn.com/trp.html): a portable, self-asserted record of teaching contribution.
- [Reflective Coach](https://www.ntlsn.com/reflective-coach.html): a Socratic scaffold across established reflective-practice models.
- [Students-as-Partners Certificate](https://www.ntlsn.com/cert.html): generate a printable student-staff partnership certificate as portfolio evidence.

## What the sector can find here
- Teaching and learning events, conferences, grants and fellowships, frameworks and policy documents, career pathways, and curated sector and international SoTL talks, all aggregated across Australian higher education.

## Open data & API
- [Open Data & API](https://www.ntlsn.com/developers.html): every dataset as open, CORS-enabled JSON — events, the 1,431-work archive, 80 best-practice guides, frameworks, and the 42-university directory. Free to query, embed via a one-line widget, or wire into any AI assistant with a copy-paste MCP server. No key, no logins.

## About and licensing
- NTLSN is a free, open Open Educational Practice commons. Paid services exist only to sustain the commons and never gate it. Curation and original benchmarking are licensed CC BY-NC-SA 4.0; linked third-party works remain the property of their authors and institutions.
- NTLSN does not claim endorsement by the universities, peak bodies, or regulators it catalogues.
